oracle_data.golden_api_signature: main(stdin: JSON object routed by action) -> stdout exactly matching public_test_cases expected_output

trigger_keywords: how to escape characters in paths
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The string '\\' must be escaped to '\\\\' and '/' must be escaped to '\/' in the output string value.
The output must contain the literal strings 'if (CKEDITOR.instances[' + element_id + ']', followed by '{', and ending with '};' to encapsulate the destruction logic safely.
The call must be made to the function 'CKEDITOR.plugins.addExternal', passing the plugin name, relative path, and filename as string arguments.

This generated package must cover the full detailed-PRD behavior represented by all authoritative public test case files, not just a subset mentioned in the fuzzy PRD. Public feature files: feature1_asset_url_normalization, feature2_widget_script_generation, feature3_content_css_and_filebrowser_urls, feature4_raw_javascript_configuration_values, feature5_auxiliary_registration_scripts, feature6_named_resource_collections, feature7_form_view_defaults_and_overrides. Feature descriptions include: Asset-backed editor resource paths are rendered as browser URLs, with cache query strings removed only where a directory-style base path is required.; Editor startup scripts render the target element id, normalized language data, selected startup mode, and input synchronization code as JavaScript.; Widget configuration converts stylesheet asset paths and browser route declarations into the final JavaScript configuration URLs.; Configuration values that are intended to be JavaScript expressions are emitted without JSON string quoting while ordinary values remain JSON encoded.; Auxiliary editor resources are rendered as JavaScript snippets that register plugins, style sets, template sets, and teardown behavior.; Named collections preserve supplied entries, replace entries with the same name, merge selected editor configuration data, and expose neutral missing-entry errors.; Creating an editor form view exposes default view variables, applies configured defaults, accepts per-form overrides, and omits editor-only variables when disabled.

The execution adapter must accept exactly the input shapes used by public_test_cases and emit stdout exactly equal to each expected_output. Observed top-level input fields/values include: action=['asset_paths', 'destroy_script', 'form_view', 'named_configs', 'plugin_script', 'read_config', 'widget_script'], assets=['{dict}'], base_path=['bundles/editor/'], js_path=['bundles/editor/editor.js'], element_id=['body'], locale_source=['request_stack'], locale=['pt_BR'], configuration=['{dict}'], options=['{dict}'], name=['missing', 'wordcount'], plugin=['{dict}'], initial=['{dict}'], operations=['[list]'], default=['default'], form_options=['{dict}'], defaults=['{dict}']. Do not require a different discriminator field, different action names, JSON output where line output is expected, or extra PASS/FAIL metadata.

The embedded public_test_cases are part of oracle_data and are authoritative for exact input shapes, action/operation names, stdout line order, whitespace, error categories, numeric formatting, and deterministic serialization. If prose, fuzzy_prd, context pointers, or older hidden constraints are vague or conflict with expected_output, the public_test_cases expected_output is the pass-critical contract.

Ignore any vague analogy or context pointer that names an unrelated module, pipeline, login flow, audit generator, or previous project. Such wording is non-normative; only this repo’s detailed PRD, FULL_SCOPE, EXECUTION_CONTRACT, and public_test_cases define the task.

context_pointers.vague_reference: apply the same stripping rule as defined in C007
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Strip trailing query strings from contentsCss paths in input.assets

context_pointers.vague_reference: handle the merge operation similarly to named_configs C020
context_pointers.actual_meaning: When 'operations' contains 'merge', the value must override the existing key while preserving other keys

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_asset_url_normalization.json.description: Asset-backed editor resource paths are rendered as browser URLs, with cache query strings removed only where a directory-style base path is required.

feature2_widget_script_generation.json.description: Editor startup scripts render the target element id, normalized language data, selected startup mode, and input synchronization code as JavaScript.

feature3_content_css_and_filebrowser_url.description: Widget configuration converts stylesheet asset paths and browser route declarations into the final JavaScript configuration URLs.

feature4_raw_javascript_configuration_va.description: Configuration values that are intended to be JavaScript expressions are emitted without JSON string quoting while ordinary values remain JSON encoded.

feature5_auxiliary_registration_scripts..description: Auxiliary editor resources are rendered as JavaScript snippets that register plugins, style sets, template sets, and teardown behavior.

feature6_named_resource_collections.json.description: Named collections preserve supplied entries, replace entries with the same name, merge selected editor configuration data, and expose neutral missing-entry errors.

feature7_form_view_defaults_and_override.description: Creating an editor form view exposes default view variables, applies configured defaults, accepts per-form overrides, and omits editor-only variables when disabled.

_constraints.output_rule: Produces line-oriented output for base_path and js_path

_constraints.input_condition: input.base_path corresponds to a directory key in input.assets or input.base_path is missing
_constraints.output_rule: The resolved URL must have trailing query strings removed (directories are prefix-only)

_constraints.input_condition: input.js_path corresponds to a file key in input.assets (does not end with /)
_constraints.output_rule: The resolved URL must be exact as returned by asset layer, including query strings
Produces the exact CSS selector from input.element_id as the first argument and a JSON object containing configuration as the second argument
Input locale values containing underscores must be normalized to lowercase hyphenated identifiers (e.g., pt_BR -> pt-br)
Include the `input_sync` flag set to true in the emitted configuration object

_constraints.input_condition: input.assets is provided with style paths
_constraints.output_rule: When resolving contentsCss from input.assets, strip trailing query strings and escape backslashes and forward slashes in the string value
Pass raw JavaScript regular expression strings directly (no JSON string quotes)

_constraints.input_condition: input.assets is provided with stylesheet paths
_constraints.output_rule: When resolving contentsCss from input.assets, strip trailing query strings

_constraints.input_condition: input.configuration.contentsCss expands to a path referencing a named resource
_constraints.output_rule: Normalize resolving the path to the environment's base path (e.g., 'theme.css' -> '/assets/theme.css')
Output must wrap the destruction logic in a safe-guard check
The element_id must be used as the instance key in the conditional check and the destroy argument
The output must include the explicit `delete` operation after the destroy call
The destroy call must pass `true` as an argument
Construct the call using strings for name, path, and filename
Query strings must be stripped from the resolved plugin base URL

_constraints.input_condition: input.action == 'named_configs' and operations is not empty
_constraints.output_rule: If 'operations' contains a 'merge' type operation, the value must override the existing key while preserving other keys

_constraints.input_condition: input.action == 'named_configs' and initial is empty and operations is empty
_constraints.output_rule: Output must indicate no entries and null for default
Output must report a missing_config error and the requested name
The error output must be on line 1 and the name on line 2
Output must be a single line starting with `view=` containing a JSON object
Include only the disabled flag in the output view

_constraints.input_condition: input.defaults does NOT include all required fields
_constraints.output_rule: When `input.defaults` is explicitly provided, output only the fields in input.defaults
Output must include the full default view object including filebrowsers, jquery_path, require_js, etc.
The output must NOT contain any other keys besides `enable`:false
